channel.title: Home on AI for Science Laboratory
channel.description: Recent content in Home on AI for Science Laboratory

item.title: Paper accepted
item.description: Our paper on data-driven wake modeling was accepted.

item.title: Data-Driven Wake Modeling
item.description: Reduced-order and learning-based methods for turbulent wake prediction.

item.title: AI for Science
item.description: Foundation and surrogate models for scientific workflows.

item.title: Publications 2026
item.description:
2026

Physics-guided wake emulation with uncertainty quantification

  • Authors: S. Yamada, K. Ito, M. Chen
  • Journal/Conference: Journal of Fluid Mechanics
  • PDF: Download PDF
  • DOI: 10.1234/jfm.2026.1001

Reliable surrogate models for coupled climate dynamics

  • Authors: H. Suzuki, E. Ford, A. Singh
  • Journal/Conference: SIAM Journal on Scientific Computing
  • PDF: Download PDF
  • DOI: 10.1234/sisc.2026.2088

item.title: Students
item.description: Doctoral and master’s students in the group.

item.title: Reduced-Order Modeling
item.description: Fast and interpretable reduced-order techniques for high-dimensional systems.

2025

  • Journal/Conference: NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks
  • PDF: Download PDF

  • Journal/Conference: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering

  • DOI: 10.1234/cma.2025.4410
